description: Pre-generated content queue for social agent. Each entry is used once then marked posted.

nostr.content: Tired of waiting 3-5 business days for payment settlements? SOL transfers settle in 400ms and cost less than a penny. Built snipelink.com to make accepting crypto as easy as sharing a link.
Hot take: every GitHub repo with a bounty program should have a payment badge in the README. We built exactly that — embeddable SVG badges that link to crypto payment pages.
Just ran npx snipelink-review on a 2-year-old codebase. Found 3 security issues and 12 performance improvements in under a minute. Free tool, no signup.
The Solana ecosystem needs better payment infrastructure for developers. Not DeFi protocols — just simple payment links that work. That's what we're building.
Freelance devs: stop paying 2.9% + 30c on every invoice. Accept USDC on Solana — instant settlement, near-zero fees. snipelink.com/@yourname
TypeScript migration doesn't have to be painful. npx snipelink-ts converts JS files with proper type inference, interfaces, and generics. Handles JSX to TSX too.
If you maintain an open source project and accept donations, you should try embeddable crypto badges. One line in your README, supports SOL/USDC/PayPal.
Published a new Dev.to article on adding crypto payment buttons to any website. 5 minutes, zero backend code. The future of web payments is peer-to-peer.
Code review tools shouldn't cost $50/mo per seat. Built a free CLI that gives you AI-powered reviews instantly: npx snipelink-review yourfile.ts
Why I built payment links for Solana instead of Ethereum: 400ms finality, $0.00025 fees, and a developer community that actually ships.

mastodon.content:
Shipped 3 free npm CLI tools for developers:

npx snipelink-review — AI code review
npx snipelink-ts — JS to TS converter
npx snipelink-readme — README generator

No accounts, no API keys. Just run them.

#DevTools #TypeScript #OpenSource

Every open source project should be able to accept tips easily. Built embeddable payment badges for GitHub READMEs — supports SOL, USDC, and PayPal.

https://snipelink.com

#OpenSource #Solana #GitHub

Quick tip: if you're migrating a JS codebase to TypeScript, try npx snipelink-ts — it infers types, generates interfaces, and handles JSX→TSX automatically.

Free, runs locally, no data sent anywhere.

#TypeScript #JavaScript #WebDev

Freelance developers: accepting crypto payments shouldn't require a blockchain degree. SnipeLink gives you a payment page in 30 seconds — share a link, get paid in SOL/USDC.

https://snipelink.com

#Freelance #Crypto #WebDev

The best developer tools are the ones you don't have to configure. That's why all our npm tools work with zero setup — just npx and go.

snipelink-review | snipelink-ts | snipelink-readme

#DevTools #DX #npm

bluesky.content: Built free payment links for devs — accept SOL, USDC, PayPal with one URL. No KYC, no monthly fees. snipelink.com
Every GitHub repo with a bounty program needs a payment badge in the README. Built that. One line of markdown, instant crypto payments.
npx snipelink-review gives you AI code review in your terminal. Free, no signup, works on any language. Try it on your worst file.
Solana devs: we built embeddable payment widgets for your apps. Dark/light theme, SOL/USDC, drop-in JS snippet. snipelink.com
TypeScript migration tip: npx snipelink-ts converts JS files with inferred types and proper interfaces. Handles React JSX to TSX too.
